assumptions: The developer has email and SMS-capable phone access.
The developer can provide an accepted payment method and billing address and accept the Master Services Agreement.
No Akamai Cloud / Linode account or compute instance exists yet.

developer_goal: Create an Akamai Cloud account, provision a first Linux virtual machine, and establish an interactive remote shell on it.

first_success.outcome: The first Linode is provisioned and fully booted, and the developer reaches its Linux shell.
first_success.observable_signal: The Linode detail page shows provisioning finished and the developer reaches a root shell via SSH Access or a Lish login prompt.

route_selection.surface: Akamai Cloud Manager self-service web flow
route_selection.rule: Get started chooses a first Linode; Distribution plus SSH is the documented general path, but plan/distribution defaults and rare manual review remain unresolved.
route_selection.selected: Cloud Manager Distribution Linode with remote shell access

candidates.name: Distribution Linode + SSH
candidates.reason: Create guide assumes Distribution; secure-setup and getting-started treat SSH as the normal first connection.

candidates.name: Distribution Linode + Lish only
candidates.reason: Also reaches a shell without local SSH keys, so lexicographic comparison to SSH remains consequential.

candidates.name: Quick Deploy App
candidates.reason: Peer source that can take longer and is not the general Distribution assumption.

candidates.name: Linode CLI or API
candidates.reason: Different surface requiring tokens/CLI before first instance.

required_path.action: Open login.linode.com/signup and create credentials with email, username, and password, or Google/GitHub signup.

required_fields.label: Password
required_fields.notes: OAuth signup prompts for username in a separate step instead of password on the first screen.

required_path.observable_result: Akamai sends a confirmation email for the new account.

required_path.action: Open the confirmation email and click the verification link.
required_path.observable_result: Email confirmation completes and signup can continue to phone verification.

required_path.action: Enter a phone number, receive the SMS code, and submit the code.
Choose Business or Personal account type, enter billing details, and accept the Master Services Agreement.

required_fields.label: Company name
required_fields.notes: Required for Business; omitted for Personal.

required_path.observable_result: Most accounts activate instantly; some enter manual review.

required_path.action: In Cloud Manager, open Create > Linode and complete region, Distribution, plan, label, and access settings, then Create Linode.

required_fields.label: Distribution
required_fields.notes: Any supported Distribution is allowed; no universal tutorial default is designated.

required_path.observable_result: Cloud Manager starts provisioning the Linode.

required_path.action: After the Linode boots, connect with the SSH Access command or open Lish to reach a shell.

required_fields.label: SSH Access command or Lish session

required_path.observable_result: The developer has an interactive root shell on the Linode.

prerequisites.description: Deliverable email and SMS-capable phone number for signup verification.
Accepted credit/debit card or PayPal plus billing address.

external_gates.description: Email confirmation and SMS phone verification are mandatory fraud-prevention gates.
Payment method and MSA acceptance are required before activation.
A minority of accounts are routed to manual review before services can be created.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email and SMS delivery must arrive during signup.
Linode image provisioning and first boot must finish before SSH or Lish succeeds.
Manual review, when triggered, delays activation by an undocumented duration.

platform_outcomes.description: Most accounts activate instantly after billing and verification.
Create Linode provisions and boots the selected Distribution image.

excluded.reason: Documentation is evidence, not a product action.

excluded.item: Post-login hardening beyond first shell access
excluded.reason: Package updates, limited sudo user, and firewall hardening are after first success.

excluded.item: Installing an application on the Linode
excluded.reason: First success is interactive shell access, not an app deploy.

sources.title: Set up and secure a Linode
Akamai free credit offer
evidence_supported: Promotional credit subject to separate rules

uncertainties.question: What evidence, duration, and decision criteria apply when signup is routed to manual review?
uncertainties.impact: A minority path adds an unbounded external gate before Create Linode.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Official documentation or support article detailing current manual-review steps and timing.

uncertainties.question: Which Distribution and plan are the universal tutorial default for first success?
uncertainties.impact: OS packages, hourly price, and capacity vary across otherwise valid first paths.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Human designation of one Distribution/plan pair as the measured default.

uncertainties.question: Is SSH Access or Lish the lexicographic shortest first-shell route after Create Linode?
uncertainties.impact: SSH may require key generation and profile upload; Lish may avoid local key setup.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Human judgment comparing documented SSH and Lish first-connection actions under SELECTION-POLICY.
